executiveSummary.text: Eutelsat has been absolutely destroyed - down ~51% from its 52-week high of тВм4.74 to тВм2.33. The stock is sitting 47% above its 52-week low of тВм1.59, having recovered from the lows but still in a severe downtrend. This is not a value opportunity in the traditional sense - it is a business under structural pressure from multiple directions. The March 2026 contract terminations for Express AT1 and AT2 (Russian satellite capacity, sanctions-related) are a fresh headwind. The тВм1.5 billion bond refina

businessModel.text: Eutelsat is a satellite operator - it owns and operates a fleet of geostationary communications satellites providing video, data, and internet connectivity services to broadcast customers, telecom operators, and enterprise clients globally. Revenue streams: Video & Broadcast (~40%): Satellite capacity for TV broadcasting - the legacy cash cow. Under pressure as viewers migrate to streaming. Government & Enterprise (~35%): Secure communications, military/government contracts, maritime and aviation connectivity. Mobile Connectivity (~15%): Backhaul for mobile networks, aviation and maritime connectivity. OneWeb LEO (~10% and growing): Low Earth Orbit constellation for broadband - the strategic bet. Operational but pre-commercial scale. Economic moat: orbital slots are scarce resources - once Eutelsat holds a geostationary slot, competitors cannot easily replicate it. Long-term contracts with broadcast clients provide revenue visibility. The moat is eroding as LEO constellations (Starlink, OneWeb) offer lower-latency alternatives.

financialSnapshot.text: Note: precise FY2026 figures from LSE/Euronext filings should be confirmed. Revenue and EBITDA guidance from H1 2025-26 results available in the February 2026 half-year report filed with AMF. Confirm from company filings before trading. Market cap: ~тВм1.1B (tiny - тВм2.33 ├Ч ~470M shares estimated) Revenue: ~тВм1.2-1.4B (confirm from filings - declining from prior years) EBITDA margin: ~65-70% historically (satellite operators have high opex leverage) Debt: Significant - the тВм1.5B bond refinancing in March 2026 addresses near-term maturities but the debt load remains the central issue for this equity Dividend: Likely suspended given financial position and debt burden PE: Not meaningful - equity is in distress territory with restructuring talk A тВм1.1B market cap on a satellite operator with тВм1.5B+ in debt is a sign of serious distress. The equity is being treated as a turnaround/restructuring story. That can work - or it can go to zero if the revenue decline accelerates faster than the debt can be reduced.

recentCatalysts.text: March 2026: тВм1.5 billion senior bond refinancing - closes near-term liquidity risk. This was a necessary move but not a transformative one. March 2026: Contract terminations for Express AT1 and AT2 satellite capacity (Russian satellites, RSCC). Sanctions-related. A revenue headwind on top of an already declining top line. February 2026: тВм1 billion financing from French export credit agency BPI for OneWeb LEO constellation - validates the strategic investment and provides non-dilutive funding for the OneWeb buildout. February 2026: H1 FY2025-26 half-year report filed with AMF - key document to review for revenue trends and EBITDA trajectory 2025-2026: Video broadcast revenue declining as European and global TV migration to streaming continues

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull (20%) OneWeb achieves commercial scale. Government contracts accelerate. Video business stabilises. Debt reduction plan credible. Satellite connectivity demand grows (IoT, maritime, defence). Stock re-rates to тВм4-6. Upside: тВм4-6. Base (45%) Gradual revenue decline. OneWeb neither fails nor flourishes in near term. Debt restructuring continues. Stock drifts sideways in тВм1.50-3.00 range. Upside: limited, downside: limited but real. Bear (35%) Video revenue accelerates decline. OneWeb burns cash faster than expected. Debt covenant breach. Equity raise at distressed price wipes out existing holders. Stock falls to тВм0.50-1.00. Downside: -55 to -75%. Expected value: (20% ├Ч тВм5.00) + (45% ├Ч тВм2.25) + (35% ├Ч тВм0.75) = тВм1.00 + тВм1.01 + тВм0.26 = ~тВм2.27 base case . Essentially at the current price - the market is pricing in exactly this outcome. The bear case has a higher probability weight than the bull. This is not a compelling risk/reward at тВм2.33.

keyRisks.text: Debt burden and refinancing risk (HIGH): The тВм1.5B refinancing addressed near-term maturities but the debt load remains enormous for a declining-revenue business. If EBITDA erodes faster than modelled, covenant breaches become a real possibility. This is the primary equity risk. Revenue decline acceleration (HIGH): Video broadcast is structural decline, not cyclical. Every year, more viewers leave satellite for streaming. Eutelsat's addressable market is shrinking. The pace of decline will determine whether the debt trajectory is manageable. OneWeb execution risk (MEDIUM-HIGH): OneWeb is the only meaningful growth lever. It is capital-intensive, pre-revenue at scale, and faces direct competition from Starlink. French export credit financing is helpful but does not guarantee commercial success. Geopolitical risk (MEDIUM): The Express AT1/AT2 terminations (Russian sanctions) illustrate this. Eutelsat has exposure to markets with geopolitical tension. European government contracts partially offset this. LEO constellation competition (MEDIUM): Starlink has dramatically more satellites deployed and a functioning commercial product. OneWeb is behind and needs to scale fast to be relevant in connectivity. Liquidity / equity raise risk (MEDIUM): A тВм1.1B market cap and тВм1.5B+ debt means the equity is thin. If the business needs more capital, an equity raise at these levels would be massively dilutive to existing holders.

risks.risk: Debt burden and refinancing risk (HIGH): The тВм1.5B refinancing addressed near-term maturities but the debt load remains enormous for a declining-revenue business. If EBITDA erodes faster than modelled, covenant breaches become a real possibility. This is the primary equity risk.

Revenue decline acceleration (HIGH): Video broadcast is structural decline, not cyclical. Every year, more viewers leave satellite for streaming. Eutelsat's addressable market is shrinking. The pace of decline will determine whether the debt trajectory is manageable.

OneWeb execution risk (MEDIUM-HIGH): OneWeb is the only meaningful growth lever. It is capital-intensive, pre-revenue at scale, and faces direct competition from Starlink. French export credit financing is helpful but does not guarantee commercial success.

Geopolitical risk (MEDIUM): The Express AT1/AT2 terminations (Russian sanctions) illustrate this. Eutelsat has exposure to markets with geopolitical tension. European government contracts partially offset this.

LEO constellation competition (MEDIUM): Starlink has dramatically more satellites deployed and a functioning commercial product. OneWeb is behind and needs to scale fast to be relevant in connectivity.

Liquidity / equity raise risk (MEDIUM): A тВм1.1B market cap and тВм1.5B+ debt means the equity is thin. If the business needs more capital, an equity raise at these levels would be massively dilutive to existing holders.

whoShouldOwn.text: Current price: тВм2.33 52-week range: тВм1.59 - тВм4.74 Distance from 52w low: +46.5% - recovered significantly from the lows Distance from 52w high: тИТ50.8% - half the peak value gone 5-day trend: тВм2.115 тЖТ тВм2.045 тЖТ тВм2.175 тЖТ тВм2.31 тЖТ тВм2.33 - attempting a bounce from the тВм2.00-2.10 support area Market cap: ~тВм1.1B - micro-cap territory for a former blue chip тВм1.59 was the 52w low - that level held in early 2026. The bounce to тВм2.33 is technically positive but the stock is still in a clear downtrend from тВм4.74. Resistance is at тВм3.00-3.20 (200-day MA and prior support) and then тВм3.70-4.00. A sustained break above тВм4.00 would be the first sign of real trend reversal. Below тВм1.75 opens to fresh lows.

recommendation.text: REDUCE. The stock is cheap on an absolute basis and the bounce from тВм1.59 to тВм2.33 is notable. But cheap is not the same as undervalued when the business trajectory is declining.

entryExit.text: REDUCE. The stock is cheap on an absolute basis and the bounce from тВм1.59 to тВм2.33 is notable. But cheap is not the same as undervalued when the business trajectory is declining. Key levels: тВм1.75-2.00: Support zone. The 52w low at тВм1.59 is the hard floor - between there and тВм2.00 is where the stock found buyers in early 2026. тВм2.00-2.50: Current range. Hold only if already in. Not a compelling entry point. тВм3.00-3.20: First meaningful resistance - 200-day MA and prior support. Would require positive news flow to reach. тВм3.50-4.00: Overhead resistance from prior support. Break above тВм4.00 is the early signal of a genuine turnaround. тВм4.00+: Would require OneWeb commercial traction proof or a structural improvement in video revenue. Not the base case. Eutelsat is a watchlist name for me - not a buy. The OneWeb angle is genuinely interesting and worth tracking. The тВм1.5B bond refinancing removes the immediate bankruptcy risk. But the equity is not yet at a level where the risk/reward justifies a position. Monitor for OneWeb commercial contract announcements or evidence of stabilising video revenue.
